Overview

Address
2901 FAIR ROAD, SIDNEY, OH 45365
Phone
9374926900
Certified beds
90
Avg daily residents
66 (73% of beds filled)
Ownership
Government (county)
Provider type
Medicare and Medicaid
Medicare/Medicaid since
2002-03-02
Setting
Rural
1 /5
★★★★★
CMS Overall Rating

CMS 5-Star Ratings

CMS rates every Medicare/Medicaid-certified nursing home on four domains. The Overall rating is driven primarily by Health Inspection results, then adjusted up or down by Staffing and Quality Measures.

Overall Rating
Composite of Health Inspection, Staffing, and Quality Measures.
★★★★★ 1/5
Health Inspection
Based on the three most recent standard surveys, with more weight on recent results.
★★★★★ 1/5
Staffing
Weighted combination of RN and total nurse staffing hours per resident per day, case-mix adjusted.
★★★★★ 2/5
Quality Measures
15 resident-level quality measures split between long-stay and short-stay (post-acute) residents.
★★★★★ 4/5
Quality Measures breakdown
Long-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 5/5
Short-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 1/5

Staffing & Workforce

Direct-care staffing is the strongest operational driver of quality in nursing homes. Values are hours per resident per day, derived from payroll-based journal (PBJ) submissions. "Case-mix" adjusts for resident acuity; "Adjusted" is the CMS rating-input value.

Role Reported Case-mix expected Adjusted Federal floor
Total nurse
All nursing staff combined: RN + LPN + Aide
 3.24 4.08 3.06 ≥ 3.48 Below floor
Registered Nurse (RN)
Licensed RN hours. Strongest driver of clinical outcomes.
 0.39 0.71 0.37 ≥ 0.55 Below floor
Licensed Practical Nurse (LPN)
LPN/LVN hours. Often handles medication administration.
 0.82 0.91 0.77 —
Nurse aide
CNA hours. Bulk of direct resident care — bathing, feeding, mobility.
 2.03 2.46 1.92 —
Licensed (RN + LPN)
Combined licensed nurse coverage.
 1.21 — — —
Physical therapist
Rehabilitation therapist hours — important for post-acute / rehab admissions.
 0.02 — — —

Federal minimums (phasing in under the CMS 2024 minimum staffing rule) shown for reference. RN: 0.55 hrs/resident/day. Total nurse: 3.48 hrs/resident/day.

Weekend staffing

Total nurse (weekend)
3.00
hrs / resident / day
RN (weekend)
0.16
hrs / resident / day
Total nurse (adjusted, weekend)
2.84
hrs / resident / day

Weekend under-staffing is a common quality-of-care concern — adverse events are more frequent when licensed coverage drops.

Staff turnover

Total nursing staff turnover
45.2%
annual
RN turnover
33.3%
annual
Administrators departed
1
last 12 months

Resident acuity

Nursing Case-Mix Index
1.45
higher acuity than avg
Case-Mix Index Ratio
1.05
facility / state ratio

Health Inspections

CMS weights three inspection cycles to compute the Health Inspection rating: the most recent (50%), the second most recent (33%), and the oldest (17%). Each standard-survey deficiency is assigned a score based on scope and severity; complaint-survey findings and revisit scores are added to produce the cycle total.

Cycle Date Total defs. Standard Complaint Deficiency score Revisits Total score
Cycle 1 (most recent) 2025-06-11 32 31 4 252 2 378
Cycle 2/3 (prior) 2022-10-20 11 3 8 64 1 64
Total weighted health score
299.5
lower is better

Deficiencies (55)

Individual survey findings. Scope/severity uses the CMS A–L matrix: letters further down the alphabet indicate greater harm and wider scope, up through J–L (immediate jeopardy).

2 A/B/C No actual harm — minor
52 D/E/F Actual harm — potential for minor
1 G/H/I Actual harm
Tag Description Scope/Severity Survey date Corrected
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. G 2025-08-14 2025-09-11
0577 Allow residents to easily view the nursing home's survey results and communicate with advocate agencies. C 2025-06-11 2025-08-14
0584 Honor the resident's right to a safe, clean, comfortable and homelike environment, including but not limited to receiving treatment and supports for daily living safely. D 2025-06-11 2025-08-14
0605 Prevent the use of unnecessary psychotropic medications or use medications that may restrain a resident's ability to function. D 2025-06-11 2025-08-14
0636 Assess the resident completely in a timely manner when first admitted, and then periodically, at least every 12 months. D 2025-06-11 2025-08-14
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. D 2025-06-11 2025-08-14
0657 Develop the complete care plan within 7 days of the comprehensive assessment; and prepared, reviewed, and revised by a team of health professionals. E 2025-06-11 2025-08-14
0658 Ensure services provided by the nursing facility meet professional standards of quality. E 2025-06-11 2025-08-14
0677 Provide care and assistance to perform activities of daily living for any resident who is unable. D 2025-06-11 2025-08-14
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. D 2025-06-11 2025-08-14
0686 Provide appropriate pressure ulcer care and prevent new ulcers from developing. D 2025-06-11 2025-08-14
0692 Provide enough food/fluids to maintain a resident's health. D 2025-06-11 2025-08-14
0693 Ensure that feeding tubes are not used unless there is a medical reason and the resident agrees; and provide appropriate care for a resident with a feeding tube. D 2025-06-11 2025-08-14
0732 Post nurse staffing information every day. C 2025-06-11 2025-08-14
0744 Provide the appropriate treatment and services to a resident who displays or is diagnosed with dementia. D 2025-06-11 2025-08-14
0755 Provide pharmaceutical services to meet the needs of each resident and employ or obtain the services of a licensed pharmacist. E 2025-06-11 2025-08-14
0756 Ensure a licensed pharmacist perform a monthly drug regimen review, including the medical chart, following irregularity reporting guidelines in developed policies and procedures. D 2025-06-11 2025-08-14
0757 Ensure each resident’s drug regimen must be free from unnecessary drugs. D 2025-06-11 2025-08-14
0759 Ensure medication error rates are not 5 percent or greater. D 2025-06-11 2025-08-14
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. F 2025-06-11 2025-08-14
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. F 2025-06-11 2025-09-11
0813 Have a policy regarding use and storage of foods brought to residents by family and other visitors. E 2025-06-11 2025-08-14
0837 Establish a governing body that is legally responsible for establishing and implementing policies for managing and operating the facility and appoints a properly licensed administrator responsible for managing the facility. F 2025-06-11 2025-08-14
0865 Have a plan that describes the process for conducting QAPI and QAA activities. F 2025-06-11 2025-08-14
0867 Set up an ongoing quality assessment and assurance group to review quality deficiencies and develop corrective plans of action. F 2025-06-11 2025-08-14
0868 Have the Quality Assessment and Assurance group have the required members and meet at least quarterly F 2025-06-11 2025-08-14
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. E 2025-06-11 2025-08-14
0881 Implement a program that monitors antibiotic use. D 2025-06-11 2025-08-14
0925 Make sure there is a pest control program to prevent/deal with mice, insects, or other pests. F 2025-06-11 2025-09-11
0550 Honor the resident's right to a dignified existence, self-determination, communication, and to exercise his or her rights. D 2025-06-11 2025-08-14
0697 Provide safe, appropriate pain management for a resident who requires such services. D 2025-06-11 2025-08-14
0803 Ensure menus must meet the nutritional needs of residents, be prepared in advance, be followed, be updated, be reviewed by dietician, and meet the needs of the resident. F 2025-06-11 2025-08-14
0610 Respond appropriately to all alleged violations. D 2024-12-30 2025-01-21
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. D 2024-12-30 2025-01-21
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. D 2024-12-30 2025-01-21
0680 Ensure the activities program is directed by a qualified professional. E 2024-10-09 2024-10-29
0755 Provide pharmaceutical services to meet the needs of each resident and employ or obtain the services of a licensed pharmacist. D 2024-10-09 2024-10-29
0755 Provide pharmaceutical services to meet the needs of each resident and employ or obtain the services of a licensed pharmacist. E 2024-02-06 2024-02-21
0759 Ensure medication error rates are not 5 percent or greater. D 2024-02-06 2024-02-21
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. D 2024-02-06 2024-02-21
0805 Ensure each resident receives and the facility provides food prepared in a form designed to meet individual needs. E 2022-10-20 2022-12-09
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. E 2022-10-20 2022-12-09
0814 Dispose of garbage and refuse properly. E 2022-10-20 2022-12-09
0607 Develop and implement policies and procedures to prevent abuse, neglect, and theft. E 2019-11-21 2020-01-10
0609 Timely report suspected abuse, neglect, or theft and report the results of the investigation to proper authorities. E 2019-11-21 2020-01-10
0610 Respond appropriately to all alleged violations. E 2019-11-21 2020-01-10
0623 Provide timely notification to the resident, and if applicable to the resident representative and ombudsman, before transfer or discharge, including appeal rights. D 2019-11-21 2020-01-10
0625 Notify the resident or the resident’s representative in writing how long the nursing home will hold the resident’s bed in cases of transfer to a hospital or therapeutic leave. D 2019-11-21 2020-01-10
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. E 2019-11-21 2020-01-10
0692 Provide enough food/fluids to maintain a resident's health. D 2019-11-21 2020-01-10

Methodology & sources
  • Nursing Home Provider Info (nh-provider-info), vintage 2026, downloaded 2026-04-14 , 14,703 rows.
  • Nursing Home Penalties (nh-penalties), vintage 2026, downloaded 2026-04-14 , 16,915 rows.
  • Nursing Home Health Deficiencies (nh-deficiencies), vintage 2026, downloaded 2026-04-14 , 418,972 rows.
  • Nursing Home Ownership (nh-ownership), vintage 2026, downloaded 2026-04-14 , 160,393 rows.
  • Skilled Nursing Facility Cost Report (snf-cost-report), vintage 2023, downloaded 2026-04-14 , 14,120 rows.

Deficiencies 0761 — description Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. 0761.description
Deficiencies 0761 — scope_severity F 0761.scope_severity
Deficiencies 0761 — survey_date 2025-06-11 0761.survey_date
Deficiencies 0761 — tag 0761 0761.tag
Deficiencies 0803 — correction_date 2025-08-14 0803.correction_date
Deficiencies 0803 — description Ensure menus must meet the nutritional needs of residents, be prepared in advance, be followed, be updated, be reviewed by dietician, and meet the needs of the resident. 0803.description
Deficiencies 0803 — scope_severity F 0803.scope_severity
Deficiencies 0803 — survey_date 2025-06-11 0803.survey_date
Deficiencies 0803 — tag 0803 0803.tag

Deficiencies 0804 — description Ensure food and drink is palatable, attractive, and at a safe and appetizing temperature. 0804.description

Deficiencies 0804 — tag 0804 0804.tag

Deficiencies 0805 — description Ensure each resident receives and the facility provides food prepared in a form designed to meet individual needs. 0805.description
